Sozom. lib . 3. cap . 15. in Africa , over and beside the bishop of Alexandria , be Didymus , a doctor of the school of Alexandria , who through occasion of a dolour that fall into his eye , become blind from his very youth : yet by continual exercise of his mind , he become excellent learn in all science : but above all thing the exact knowledge of divine scripture make he a terror to the arrian . Symmachus . to anastatius succeed Symmachus , in the day of the emperor anastatius : and when Theodoricus king of Goth reign , in Italy , great sedition be among the people , at his election . the one part of the clergy and people choose Symmachus , the other Laurentius to be bishop of Rome : but with common consent , platina de vit . pontiff . a synod be appoint at Ravenna , and there the election of Symmachus be ratify , he continue in office . 15 year , 6 month , 22. day . Horm•sda the successor of Symmachus sit 9 year , 18. day : who by commandment of Theodoricus king of Goth , and reign in Italy , gather a council at room and damn the error of Eutyches of new again . likewise ambassador be send to the Emp. Anastatius , and to John B. of Constantinople to exhort they to forsake the wicked error of Eutyches , and to acknowledge two nature in Christ , to wit , the divine and humane nature . but anastatius answer with proud word , nos imperare volumus , nobis imperari nolumus . that be we will command , but we will not be command . likewise the B. of Constantinople puff up in pride by the assistance of the Emp. despise the counsel of Hermisda . moreover against the law of nation , they deal in humanly with the ambassador of Hormisda , Platin. de vit . Pontif. and thrust they into a old and break ship , with strait commandment that they shall not arrive at any harbour in Grecia , note . but keep a direct course towards Italy . notwithstanding by the providence of God that ship arrive safe at the coast of Italy . the error of the manichaeans begin again to be overspread in room . but Hormisda take their book and burn they in the porch of the church call Constantina . John the first govern the church of room in the day of Iustinus the elder , John 2. to who also he be send ambassador by Theodoricus , to crave that the arrian bishop who he have banish out of his dominion may be restore to their place again : else the catholic bishop of Italy shall expect all kind of rigor at his hand . the B. Viii . of the nature and kind of the union between Christ and the faithful , and of the fruit and effect arise from the same . union be a make of many into one . now there be sundry kind of union : there be a consubstantial union ( as Bernard call it ) in the divinity ; but this so transcendent , as it may be call rather unity than union , and rather one than unity . the father , the word , and the spirit , these three be one , 1. Ioh. 5. 7. and Christ say , i and the father be one ; not unite , but one , Ioh. 10. 30. so that this union in the divinity , this unity , this one , have no parallel . as Bernard say , speak of some other union : haec omnia , quid ad illud summum , at { que } Bern. be consid . lib . 5. cap . 8. ( ut ita dicam ) vnicè unum , ubi vnitatem consubstantialitas facit ? all other union , what be they to that one supreme , and ( as i may so say ) that only one , where consubstantiasity make the unity ? And , super Cantica , sermon . 71. singularis ac summa illa est vnitas , quae non vnitione constat , sed extat aeternitate : that be the most singular and excellent unity , which consist not by unition , but exist by eternity . there be also a personal union , and that be of the two nature in Christ ; which Bernard call dignatiua vnitas , qua limus noster à dei verbo in unam assumptus est personam : a vouchsafe or gracious unity , whereby the word of God vouchsafe to assume our slimy nature into the unity of his person . there be a sacramental union between the sign , and the thing signify in the sacrament . there be a natural or animal union of the soul and body in man . there be a accidental union between the mind and learning , find in a learned man . there be a artificial union between the hand and the instrument ; as when the work be predicate of , or denominate of they both joint : as a carve work imply both the hand and tool wherewith it be wrought . there be a moral union between two friend , as David and Jonathan . there be a civil union between the prince and the people . there be a union of dependency between the creature and the creator ; for in he we live , and move , and have our being , act 17. 28. final ( to pass by other ) there be a spiritual and mystical union between Christ and believer : which be call spiritual , especial , from the principal efficient of it , the spirit of God , and of Christ ; as the apostle declare , 1. Cor. 12. 13. by one spirit be we all baptize into one mystical body of Christ . now this spiritual union between Christ and the believer , as it come short of that first transcendent union in the sacred Trinity in unity , so it do as far excel all those other union ; yet so , as it seem to partake in some thing of they all . for first , concern that stupendous and wondrous union in the divine Hypostaces or person , our union with Christ be resemble to it : as Ioh. 17. 20. 21. neither pray i for these alone , but for they also which shall believe on i through their word ; that they may all be one , as thou father art in i , and i in thou , that they also may be one in we and , Ioh. 14. 20. at that day you shall know that i be in the father , and you in i , and i in you . yea , Christ and his believer be so unite in one , in one mystical body , as Christ and they be call one Christ , 1. Cor. 14. 12. So be Christ , that be , Christ and all his member ; be there compare to one body compact of many member : so be Christ , say the apostle . so then , as the father be in the son , and the son in the father , one God ; so believer be in Christ , and Christ in believer , one Christ . so that the union between the father and the son , and between Christ and we , seem to be alike . it be somewhat like indeed , but nothing alike : for the father and Christ be one ; so be Christ and the believer one , but yet in different respect . the father and the son be one , but essential and natural : Christ and the believer be one , not essential nor natural , but be make so by grace , as Ioh. 17. 23. that they may be make perfect in one . so 2. Pet. 1. 4. we be make partaker of the divine nature by gift . and as Bernard say ; hanc vnitatem nontam essentiarum cohaerentia facit , Bern. super Cant. ser . 71. quam continentia voluntatum : this unity be wrought , not so much by the coherency of essence , as by the correspondency and nearness of will . and again ; homini and deo , sva cni { que } and natura , and substantia est , cum patris filij { que } co•stet penitus esse unam : in the union between God and man , each of they notwithstanding retain their nature and substance proper to themselves ; but the father and the son have both one and the same substance . so that in our unity with God in Christ , there be not confusio naturarum , sed voluntatum consensio : not a confusion of nature , but a consent of will . second , this union between Christ and the believer , be not a hypostatical or personal union , such as be between the two nature in Christ ; but it be mystical only , and such as make the believer in Christ , to be with he one Christ , yet not personal , but spiritual and mystical , as 1. Cor. 6. 17. he that be join unto the lord , be one spirit . third , this union between Christ and the believer , be not that sacramental union between the sign and the thing signify , sith the sign have no benefit from the thing signify , nor be it any long a sign , than in the sacramental use and application to the believe communicant , and so the sacramental union cease ; yet as unto every faithful receiver , wheresoever the visible sign be administer , the invisible grace signify be together exhibit , by virtue of the sacramental union , have dependence on Christ promise , and reference to the condition of faith in the communicant : so such be the union between Christ and the believer , that wheresoever faith be , there also be Christ with all his grace present to the believer ; for he dwell in our heart by faith , Ephes. 3. 17. four , this union between Christ and the believer , be not natural ( or native , as Bernard call it ) as that between the soul and the body in man ; because the one of they may be separate from the other by death ; but Christ and the believer be never separate , no not in death : for to i to live , be Christ , and to dye be gain , Phil. 1. 21. for who shall separate we from the love of Christ ?
